tweets.title: BREAKING: Sheikh Hasina Plans Return to Bangladesh Former Bangladeshi Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina says she plans to return to Bangladesh around December with senior Awami League leaders and voluntarily surrender to authorities despite facing a death sentence. Hasina, currently in exile in India, said she is prepared to be arrested, stating: “If death comes, I want it to come on my own soil.” She said the move is intended to challenge the legal proceedings against her and protest what she describes as the repression of Awami League members.
tweets.content:
BREAKING: Sheikh Hasina Plans Return to Bangladesh

Former Bangladeshi Prime Minister Sheikh Hasina says she plans to return to Bangladesh around December with senior Awami League leaders and voluntarily surrender to authorities despite facing a death sentence.

Hasina, currently in exile in India, said she is prepared to be arrested, stating:

“If death comes, I want it to come on my own soil.”

She said the move is intended to challenge the legal proceedings against her and protest what she describes as the repression of Awami League members.

tweets.content:
Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
10th Jul 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● Indian-flagged container ship DP WORLD GODAVARI crossed Strait of Hormuz under apparent US air cover yesterday and is currently near Dubai, heading toward Jebel Ali anchorage.

● The IDF located weapons facilities in the Security Zone in southern Lebanon, containing launchers, machine guns, explosive devices, and missiles belonging to Hezbollah.

● JMIC said that the expanded southern transit corridor through the Strait of Hormuz remains open despite recent attacks on ships along the US supported route through Omani waters.

● Qatar’s PM and FM called for full implementation of the US-Iran MoU, including guarantees for freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz.

● Iranian lawmaker Sabeti called on Parliament to fast track legislation targeting Trump and other US and Israeli officials.

END OF THE BRIEF

Netram Defence Review (@NetramDefence)

Daily US-Israel-Iran War Brief:
9th Jul 2026:

- We will be posting daily war updates on the ongoing war in Middle East
- ⁠Follow us for daily Brief

● Iran had launched a coordinated wave of drone & missile attacks in the Gulf as a response to recent US airstrikes on Iran. The strikes hit Patriot defence systems in Kuwait, an early-warning radar site in Qatar, and fuel storage facilities in Bahrain.

● Iranian missiles triggered air raid sirens in Jordan after being directed toward a US base in the country.

● IRGC Navy declared that foreign forces have no rights in the Strait of Hormuz or Iranian territory. It warned that any US military intervention aimed at controlling shipping lanes would face a decisive response.

● Qatar paused efforts to rapidly revive production at the world’s largest LNG facility after an attack on one of its tankers in the Strait of Hormuz raised fear.

● Iranian officials report that U.S. strikes hit the perimeter of the Bushehr Nuclear Power Plant. The facility was one of several locations targeted in Bushehr province.

● Only three fuel tankers were seen transiting the Strait of Hormuz on Thursday as US-Iran hostilities intensified, according to vessel-tracking data.

END OF THE BRIEF

India and Australia have signed a long-term agreement for the export of Australian uranium to support India's civilian nuclear energy programme.

- Supports India's goal of achieving 100 GW of nuclear power capacity by 2047
- Strengthens the India–Australia strategic partnership in energy, defence, and critical minerals

Australia holds around 28% of the world's known uranium reserves, making the deal a significant step in securing fuel for India's expanding nuclear energy sector.

* ਜਿਨ੍ਹਾਂ ਨੇ ਨਿਰਦੋਸ਼ ਲੋਕਾਂ ਨੂੰ ਬੱਸਾਂ 'ਚੋਂ, ਬਾਜ਼ਾਰਾਂ 'ਚੋਂ ਕੱਢ ਕੇ ਗੋਲੀਆਂ ਦਾ ਨਿਸ਼ਾਨਾ ਬਣਾਇਆ।

* ਸ਼ਾਮ ਦੇ 5 ਵੱਜਦੇ ਹੀ ਪੂਰੇ ਪੰਜਾਬ 'ਚ ਸੰਨਾਟਾ ਪਸਰ ਜਾਂਦਾ ਸੀ। ਮਾਵਾਂ ਦੇ ਲਾਲ, ਭੈਣਾਂ ਦੇ ਵੀਰ ਇੱਕ ਝਟਕੇ 'ਚ ਖਤਮ ਕਰ ਦਿੱਤੇ ਜਾਂਦੇ ਸਨ।

* ਅੱਜ ਵੀ ਉਨ੍ਹਾਂ ਪਰਿਵਾਰਾਂ ਦੇ ਬੱਚੇ ਉਹ ਖੌਫਨਾਕ ਰਾਤਾਂ ਯਾਦ ਕਰਕੇ ਕੰਬ ਜਾਂਦੇ ਨੇ।

* ਇਹ ਸੀ 90 ਦਾ ਦਹਾਕਾ—ਖੂਨ ਨਾਲ ਲਿਖਿਆ ਇਤਿਹਾਸ। ਚੱਲੀ ਹੋਈ ਗੋਲੀ ਨੇ ਕਦੇ ਵੀ ਹਿੰਦੂ ਜਾਂ ਸਿੱਖ ਨਹੀਂ ਦੇਖਿਆ।

* ਹਥਿਆਰਾਂ ਅਤੇ ਹਿੰਸਾ ਦਾ ਦਿਖਾਵਾ ਕਦੇ ਵੀ ਪੰਜਾਬ ਦਾ ਭਵਿੱਖ ਨਹੀਂ ਹੋ ਸਕਦਾ।

* ਅੱਜ ਪੰਜਾਬ ਨੂੰ ਹਥਿਆਰਾਂ ਦੀ ਨਹੀਂ, ਸਗੋਂ ਸ਼ਾਂਤੀ, ਤਰੱਕੀ ਅਤੇ ਭਾਈਚਾਰਕ ਸਾਂਝ ਦੀ ਲੋੜ ਹੈ। 🕊️🌾

* ये 90 के दशक की तस्वीरें हैं, आज के किसी प्री-वेडिंग फोटोशूट की नहीं।
* AK-47 और ड्रम मैगजीन लेकर घूमने वाले ये कौन थे, जिनके सिरों पर लाखों का इनाम था?
* जिन्होंने निर्दोष लोगों को बसों और बाज़ारों से निकालकर गोलियों का निशाना बनाया।
* शाम के 5 बजते ही पूरे पंजाब में सन्नाटा पसर जाता था। माताओं के लाल, बहनों के भाई एक झटके में खत्म कर दिए जाते थे।
* आज भी उन परिवारों के बच्चे वो खौफनाक रातें याद करके कांप जाते हैं।
* यह था 90 का दशक—खून से लिखा इतिहास। चली हुई गोली ने कभी हिंदू या सिख नहीं देखा।
* हथियारों और हिंसा का दिखावा कभी भी पंजाब का भविष्य नहीं हो सकता।
* आज पंजाब को हथियारों की नहीं, बल्कि शांति, तरक्की और आपसी भाईचारे की जरूरत है।

* These are pictures from the 90s, not from some modern-day pre-wedding photoshoot.
* Who were these people roaming around with AK-47s and drum magazines, carrying bounties of lakhs on their heads?
* The ones who pulled innocent people out of buses and markets to make them targets of their bullets.
* As soon as the clock struck 5 PM, a dead silence would spread across Punjab. Sons of mothers and brothers of sisters were wiped out in an instant.
* Even today, the children of those families tremble at the memory of those terrifying nights.
* This was the 90s—a history written in blood. A fired bullet never differentiated between a Hindu or a Sikh.
* The display of weapons and violence can never be the future of Punjab.
* Today, Punjab does not need weapons; it needs peace, progress, and brotherhood.
